task_yaml.task:
I have a messy Obsidian research vault here:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/project_vault/

It was imported from a small snapshot of the public Vite documentation
Markdown on GitHub. The raw import kept many docs-style relative links,
has weak Obsidian organization, and may include a repeated page from a
review queue. Please open it in the real Obsidian desktop app and
clean it up. Do not delete the substantive Vite docs content or the
provenance comments; copy a cleaned version to:

    /tmp_workspace/results/project_vault_cleaned/

The cleaned vault must include:
- an `INDEX.md` that links to the main Vite guide/config/plugin notes
- normalized tags for guide/config/plugin/build/deploy topics
- fixed local internal links where the imported target note exists
- documented unresolved links where the target page was not imported
- `duplicates.md` listing duplicate or near-duplicate imports and your
  keep/merge recommendation
- `orphans.md` listing notes that still have no clear incoming links

Also write a cleanup report at:

    /tmp_workspace/results/vault_cleanup_report.md

The report should include note count inspected, broken or non-Obsidian
links found and fixed/documented, duplicate groups found, tag
normalization before/after, and unresolved issues. Save an Obsidian
screenshot showing the cleaned vault or index note at:

    /tmp_workspace/results/obsidian_cleanup.png

Please actually use Obsidian for the review/screenshot; command-line
file edits can help, but the GUI evidence should show the final vault.
